_provenance.method: Each section's model, subsections, tone, placeholders, and word_count were extracted directly from the corresponding prompt in SKILL.md. No content was invented — only structure was captured.

_adaptation_truth.what_is_real: SKILL.md is vendored verbatim. This manifest captures its structure for programmatic consumption.
_adaptation_truth.what_is_adapted: The manifest is a derived artifact — it does not contain the full prompt text, only structural metadata.
_adaptation_truth.what_is_missing: The full prompt text for each section is only in SKILL.md. For LLM-based drafting, read SKILL.md directly.
_adaptation_truth.consumption: drafting.py reads this manifest at runtime to derive section skeletons.

abstract.tone: direct, precise, dense with information, Q1 journal standard
abstract.tense: past for completed studies, present for ongoing/proposed
subsections: 1-2 sentences establishing research problem and why it matters
Main objectives or research questions
Methodology: study design, participants, key procedures
Most important findings with specificity (key statistics if applicable)
1-2 sentences on implications for theory, practice, or policy
rules: No vague statements like 'results are discussed'

introduction.tone: human-like, conversational academic, varied sentence lengths, natural pauses
subsections: Introduce topic and explain real-world or theoretical issue
Problem statement: ideal situation vs current shortfall
Previous attempts and why they don't solve the problem
Direct and indirect results of the problem and consequences
Knowledge gap this study fills and why this approach is necessary
Key studies: how work builds on or differs from them
Theory or conceptual model guiding the study
Objectives of the study
CARS model paragraph: establish territory, identify niche, occupy niche
model_steps: Establish territory — why the topic matters
Identify niche — what is missing or uncertain in current research
Occupy niche — purpose and direction of study

literature_review.tone: formal academic style, logical flow, Q1 journal standard
subsections: Broad topic statement and significance
Critical synthesis of most relevant literature
Examine each study: aim, method, findings, limitations
Identify patterns, contradictions, knowledge gaps
Assess overall condition of the literature
How your research addresses the gap

methods.tone: clear, detailed, human-like academic, past tense, sufficient for replication
subsections: Study design and justification
Research setting and timeframe
Ethics statement: approval, institution, informed consent
Participants or subjects: population, sampling, inclusion/exclusion, demographics
Equipment and materials: tools, devices, substances with justification
Study procedures: chronological, interventions, data collection
Outcome measures: primary and secondary outcomes
Statistical analysis: tests, significance level, power analysis, software

results.tone: careful researcher presenting evidence, not machine listing numbers
subsections: Restate research questions or hypotheses
Descriptive statistics or demographic data
Main analyses: test statistics, df, p-values, effect sizes
Secondary analyses
Negative or non-significant results (honest reporting)
Excluded data and reasons
rules: Describe what data shows without interpretation
Reference tables and figures without duplicating content

discussion.tone: intelligent, natural, human academic, clear transitions, broad paragraphs
subsections: Critical examination relating findings to previous literature
Comparison with previous researchers: agreement, contradiction, novelty
Impact on theory, policy, and practice
Limitations and how they might have affected findings
Precise recommendations for future research

conclusion.tone: academic, coherent, human-like, Q1 journal publication standard
subsections: Restate main purpose and objectives
Briefly summarize key findings from results section
Broader significance for theory, practice, and policy
Implications for future research, practice, decision-making
Reflect on limitations and how to address in future studies
Forward-looking statement advancing understanding
